title: [lichen_l2.c:507-543] SECURITY: O(n) signature verification per frame enables DoS
description:
Security finding: peer_try_all_pubkeys() iterates ALL peers for EVERY received frame.

Analysis:
1. For each RX frame, loop runs CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_MAX_NEIGHBORS times (default 16)
2. Each iteration calls lichen_link_rx() which includes Schnorr-48 verify
3. Schnorr-48 verify is computationally expensive (scalar mult on Ed25519)
4. Malformed frames still traverse full loop before failing

Attack scenario:
1. Attacker floods malformed frames at maximum LoRa rate
2. Each frame causes 16 Schnorr-48 verifications before rejection
3. CPU is saturated processing malicious frames
4. Legitimate frames are dropped due to queue overflow

At SF10 BW125:
- ~6.8s per 255-byte frame on air
- Attacker limited by channel capacity, not verification speed
- But shorter frames (header only) transmit faster

Impact: P2 - limited by LoRa data rate. At SF10, attack throughput is low.

Mitigation:
1. Parse frame ONCE, extract signature, verify against all pubkeys
   (Current code redundantly parses for each pubkey)
2. Add rate limiting per source EUI-64
3. Use bloom filter for quick rejection of unknown sources
4. Accept as acceptable risk given LoRa throughput limits
close_reason: Documented: architecture limitation - O(n) verification is acceptable because LoRa data rate limits attack throughput to ~9 frames/min at SF10, making CPU DoS impractical.
